name: Braised Duck (or Mussels) w. Red Curry
recipeIngredient: Salt and freshly ground black pepper -- to taste
1/4 cup rendered duck fat or vegetable oil
5 cups brown duck stock or chicken stock
3 tablespoons palm sugar or brown sugar
1 bunch scallions: white & 1/2 green part only -- trimmed & thinly sliced along diagonal
recipeInstructions:
1. Bone the ducks and remove the skin. Ask a butcher to do this, reserving the carcass for stock making and skin for rendering. Cut breasts in half, then into about 3 pieces across the width. Chop legs and thighs into about 4 pieces each.
2. Sprinkle meat with salt and pepper. Heat rendered fat or vegetable oil in a large, heavy-bottomed Dutch oven over high heat. Brown duck on all sides, then transfer meat to a platter. Reduce heat to medium-low and cook shallots until well browned, about 15 minutes.
3. Add garlic and ginger, increase heat to medium-high and cook for about a minute. Then add curry paste and cook, stirring constantly, for about 3 minutes.
4. Return duck meat to the pot along with brown duck stock or chicken stock. Cook at a slow simmer, uncovered, until meat is tender, about 40 minutes. With a slotted spoon, transfer meat to a platter and reserve in a warm place.
5. Puree the sauce in a blender and strain back into the pot. Cook over high heat until the liquid is reduced by one-third. Stir in fish sauce, coconut milk, sugar, and lime juice, then remove from heat. Stir duck back into warm sauce. Ladle stew over jasmine rice. Garnish with remaining ingredients and serve immediately.
6. Yield: 6 servings
7. Variation: RED CURRY WITH MUSSELS
- Use 4 pounds of fresh mussels, washed and debearded
- Follow the above recipe, substituting vegetable oil instead of duck fat and fish stock in place of duck stock
- After cooking the sauce, add the mussels along with the stock and simmer until shells open. After transferring to a platter, remove mussels from shells and stir into warm sauce.
